relative_path: 4. Meetings & Updates/Meeting_notes/2025/02.09.2025 GeoMx preprocessing and labeling.md
full_text:
Zhihan explained his recent advances in preprocessing for the data and
exploration of GeoMx results. Main comments:

- UMAPs from batch corrected data display intermix of immune
 > aggregates and GC AOIs

- Gene-level exploration with boxplots shows distinct BCL-6 +/- groups
 > in GC AOIs, which might be linked to some aggregates being labeled
 > as GC.

- We need to explore and verify the identity of the AOIs before diving
 > deeper

[TO-DO:]{.underline}

- **Creating a ppt/word to put together manuscript-like
 > ideas/figures**

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
- **1. Figures displaying patient-level and region-level AOI data:**

 - a\) Figure displaying the sampling procedure

 - b\) Summary statistics of the results

A) Ada suggests making a heatmap with this, where the C- x axis ticks
 > would be our samples, HR status could be shown upwards, and the Rs
 > would be the regions (Stroma,
 > GC...)[[https://jokergoo.github.io/ComplexHeatmap-reference/book/heatmap-annotations.html]{.underline}](https://jokergoo.github.io/ComplexHeatmap-reference/book/heatmap-annotations.html).
 > Intersection should show number of AOIs from a given patient and a
 > given region.This can be done in R

![](media/image1.png){width="2.651042213473316in"
height="2.523870297462817in"}

- **2. Re-checking labels** (HIGHEST PRIORITY) -

**IMPORTANT:** BEFORE RUNNING, **VERIFY THIS** **HAS BEEN RE-SCALED

g){width="2.651042213473316in"
height="2.523870297462817in"}

- **2. Re-checking labels** (HIGHEST PRIORITY) -

**IMPORTANT:** BEFORE RUNNING, **VERIFY THIS** **HAS BEEN RE-SCALED
[AFTER]{.underline} SUBSETTING THE TLS DATA OUTSIDE OF BATCH3!!**

- a\) Getting a genelist/signature associated with GC B cells

 - Our pre-print, consulting Joona on current literature etc

- b\) Plotting those in violin plots, while also displaying:

 - Biological order in x-axis (Stroma, Aggregates, T-Bcell, GC)

 - See whether in aggregates/gc you have separate distributions

- c\) Heatmaps could also help at this stage

 - You can also plot the genes as heatmap vs the AOIs and their
 > annotations and see how they cluster. It is important to trace
 > which AOIs and from which patients they are. You can create
 > tentative labels out of the clustering results (eg if you have
 > some aggs clustering with GCs, you can try to assign those as
 > GC as well)

- d\) UMAP: Replot the umap, does it look any better?

 - You can also run the UMAP again only with the B-cell genes you
 > selected.

 - Ada highly encourages also trying to PCA with all genes, and
 > check which components explain B-cell genes, then plotting
 > those PCs. This will give you a better plot.

- e\) From the AOIs that swapped labels, go back to the image and see
 > if the new/old labels match with the morphology

 -
